transcript:
Welcome everyone. This is our first Breakpoint here in the Middle East. Uh, it is our fir- our fifth Breakpoint. Uh, we did our first three in Europe. Last year we were in Asia, and this is our first, certainly not our last here in the UAE. First, why are we here in the UAE? Uh, well, uh, here in the UAE, uh, the UAE tends to see technological trends early and commit at national scale. One example of this was in 2017 when most countries were not paying attention, the UAE had, uh, a- had its first appointment of a Ministry of AI, and those early commitments are paying off dividends today. What we see here in the UAE is a one gigawatt, uh, supercomputing facility, 200 megawatts of data center capacity being built out. Uh, and these are not just press releases. This is real industrial capacity. And this is what mirrors our spirit of building within Solana as well. We also ship in production. We ship products and we ship at real scale, real capital, uh, and with a real community around us. Um, and, uh, and so this is one of the reasons why we've brought Solana here to the UAE. Uh, and, uh, and we are really excited to be here. Uh, this is our largest Breakpoint ever, and handing it over to Akshay to talk more about that.

All right. Well, this is the biggest Breakpoint ever. Five years in a row. We have over 7,000 attendees for this Breakpoint. People flying in from all over the world, over 105 countries. The longest flight is I think from California, something like, uh, 16 hours. So all our friends from California, thank you so much for coming all this distance. And of course, everybody else who took a flight to be here. Uh, it is an incredible honor to host this wonderful gathering. You know, all of us are terminally online and it's always nice when we can download the entire social network to a physical location once a year. We have 1,700 founders, you know, establishing Solana as truly the Silicon Valley of the internet. And, uh, you know, you'll see stats of where they are from around the world as we wrap up Breakpoint. But it's truly a global community.

All right. So, uh, let's talk a- let's talk about the State of Solana in 2025. Solana is not just one company. It's not just one product. Solana means many different things to many different people. To, uh, validators and core developers, Solana is a network. To application builders, Solana is a technology platform. It is infrastructure for applications. To asset issuers and investors, Solana is capital markets. Uh, and for Solana as an ecosystem to really thrive, each three of these parts of the network, uh, the network, the application layer, and also the capital markets part of Solana all need to thrive. All need to be profitable. So let's dive in to each part of that. Starting with the network. This is the bedrock, of course, of everything that happens here in Solana. Uh, and a lot of people know that Solana is fast and cheap. That's probably one of the top things that people know. Uh, but what we are committed to here is always becoming faster and cheaper. We call that IBRL: increase bandwidth, reduce latency. IBRL is what broke the blockchain trilemma. And we are constantly pushing boundaries. Uh, on 10/10, when centralized exchanges around the world melted down, 20 billion dollars of liquidations in just two hours, Solana didn't break a sweat. We cracked 100,000 transactions per second, uh, and, uh, were able to process 60 million compute units, uh, in a- in a block. That was all handled by Agave. And what we have also been able to achieve is over a million transactions per section- uh, in a lab setting with the Agave client. We've talked a lot about in the past, Firedancer could get to a million. It's not just one core client now. It is both. Another big innovation this year has been from Jito, which launched the Block Assembly Marketplace. So with BAM, applications can control their order sequencing, which is really important to be able to, for example, have oracle updates on demand, be able to cancel orders without spamming the network, and to also pass value to builders, not to extractors. Uh, TradFi runs on dedicated bandwidth. Uh, dedicated fiber around the world just to process transactions. And Solana now has that too with DoubleZero, which launched earlier this year. We now have 3.3 terabits per second of capacity dedicated to Solana validators and RPC nodes. Uh, and 40% of Solana's network is now running on DoubleZero. And lastly, much anticipated this Breakpoint, come hear from Agave about Alpenglow and what the plans are for multiple concurrent leaders.

All right. And then the next layer of the stack is of course applications. If you remember and attended Breakpoint last year, you know that we made a very opinionated bet to make the entire conference around applications when the entire industry was largely focused on infrastructure. And thanks to our folks and friends at Blockworks, I don't know where they are, give them a shout out, they really advanced the adoption of this application-centric focus by shipping data that actually matters to the industry. And, you know, we've generated, uh, this is truly a year of all-time highs. All-time highs in app revenue, all-time highs in REV. Um, all-time highs in total developers in the network, total new developers in the network, all-time highs in spot DEX volume, all-time highs in new tokens launched, all-time highs and of course market cap earlier this year. And truly a testament for how this community that meets once a year is shipping stuff into production and winning in, uh, an adversarial business environment. And so, uh, one of the best charts I saw yesterday, which we thought we should include here today, shout out to Solana Legend, is that, you know, the- the companies growing to a hundred million dollars in revenue are the fastest growing companies are all are mostly in crypto and most of them are on Solana. So when we say that Solana is the Silicon Valley of the internet, that is reflected in the graph- in this graph here and the performance of teams shipping, uh, shipping exceptional applications. You know, there used to be two garage- like a- you know, two folks in a garage and these garage startups in Silicon Valley. Then the garage got- the garages got too expensive. But guess what? They move- migrated to Solana and now they're, you know, two or three person teams from around the world shipping apps that, uh, gain a hundred million dollars in market cap- uh, a hundred million dollars revenue and billions of dollars in market cap. And so we're so honored that this community sees Solana as a network to ship that.

All right. And now let's talk about capital. This was a year that, uh, institutions were unblocked by regulation. We as Solana were the first blockchain to start a policy institute, the Solana Policy Institute. And now it is imperative for every institution to have a digital asset strategy. And as those institutions come into blockchain, they are choosing Solana. Western Union processes over a hundred billion dollars of remittance volume every year. They chose Solana. Fiserv processes two trillion dollars every year of merchant payment volume, and they chose Solana as well. Of course, ETFs have been a big theme this year. Finally, we got physical staked ETFs. Uh, they came onto Solana in force about six weeks ago, and in that six week time, we're almost at one billion dollars of AUM. We had three straight, uh, weeks of inflows when the market was doing the opposite. And here are the six physical staked ETFs that are listed just in the US market alone. Of course, there's another big theme this year, a little bit controversial: DATs. A lot of people see DATs as short-term liquidity vehicles, but we think it is the opposite because Solana is one of the few places where you can build a company on the infrastructure and you can also build a company on the asset. So we think about DATs as being long-term ecosystem companies that are going to bridge Solana to public markets, to build infrastructure, asset management, and bring all of that into a single vehicle. And of course, one of our strongest DATs is here in the UAE: Solmate.

All right. So the theme for this year's Breakpoint. We're going to walk through some of the logistics, things you should be aware of, how to make the most of your Breakpoint experience. The themes we picked are Revenue and Returns. Rev- because we think teams shipping applications, it's not- it's not sufficient like it was last year to just ship applications anymore. We need applications that generate revenue so they can become self-sustaining and grow o- uh, on the, you know, strength of their own balance sheet into- into the future. And so we prioritize teams that generate revenue or teams that help other teams generate revenue. So that's one core set of, like, uh, um, uh, our audience, which are founders, developers, folks shipping tooling. And then another core set of course are folks generating alpha, beta, gamma, and all the great Greek letters on our network. And so if they're investors gen- or traders, individual investors, uh, family offices, hedge funds, venture funds, in- uh, you know, trade- prop shops, uh, all generate, uh, various, you know, sort of, uh, uh, return generation strategies on Solana. And we wanted to have, uh, a focus for them in this conference. And so those are the two, uh, uh, those are the two key- key themes for this year that you can expect. And we obviously have two stages we'll walk through in a short second.

Okay. So let's talk through our most prominent format. We've got five-minute product keynotes. Uh, as you know, we eliminated panels last year. People loved it. We're doing that again. Uh, so we've got a, uh, whole series of five-minute product keynotes where people ship all year and, uh, and come here to make their product announcement. Uh, I'm just going to highlight a few. I won't ruin it. Please go to the talks. Um, we've got Coinbase talking about building at the intersection of centralized exchanges and decentralized exchanges. DFlow, uh, is launching their new router. Erebor is a, uh, is a digital asset bank, uh, dedicated to, um, American innovation. We've got Ellipsis, long awaited, uh, building next generation perps on Solana. Be sure to check that one out. Sunrise is, uh, asset onboarding, uh, bridge to bring all assets onto Solana. And then MetaDAO, uh, this is internet capital markets at its core. Uh, and so go check it out.

All right. The next format we have are debates. If you remember last year we introduced the debate format because, uh, one of the core cultural tenets of Solana is having a har- having the ability to have hard conversations that involve trade-offs. You know, there's no holy grail in Solana. Like the- we- we thrive on debates, you know, online and offline. And we wanted to platform the best ones, uh, of the year. And so you'll see some of the faces you recognize here debated out. You know, um, and we- we, you know, f- to give you an example, this house rejects political alignment as a growth strategy for crypto, as we've seen perhaps in the context of the US. This house believes that L1s are overvalued, something that you've been seeing on X lately. And of course, what model of tokenized securities is the one that wins out. And so you'll have exceptional speakers debated out. It's a two-on-two, uh, team sport this year. We got rid of the moderator because they were trying to achieve consensus too quickly. And so it'll make for, uh, a much more interesting dialogue. So be sure to check that out.

All right. And lastly, we've got fireside chats. Fireside chats are discussions between industry peers. Of course, uh, Raj and Toly, our founders, are going to be on stage talking about the future of Solana. Uh, we've also got a fireside between Victor and Marco from Solmate. Uh, you might have heard that they recently merged, uh, and now collectively Solana- uh, Solmate owns two billion dollars worth of assets. So they're going to be talking about merging infrastructure, asset management, and market making. Uh, and also be sure to check out the fireside chat with J.P. Morgan.

All right. We've got two stages. The stage you're on is called Absolute Cinema. Named appropriately, hopefully. All right. Okay. Crowd's gone quiet. There we go. Um, this is- and then on the- on the first floor we have a Lock-In Stage. They're actually pretty cl- uh, they, you know, they tell you what they exactly are. The Absolute Cinema Stage is meant for tea- for us to go wide on the ecosystem. There are five to ten minute formats to give you a sense for what is happening across the broad swath of all the verticals Solana has from payments to DePIN to trading, um, to tokenization to- and all the other good stuff. And then a Lock-In is a stage to go deep. So we have three days of programming. Day 1 is focused on the institutional adoption of Solana. You know, this has been a large theme for this year. And so we have programming for that on this stage as well as on the Lock-In Stage, uh, and which is on the first floor. Day 2 is dedicated to trading, uh, related applications because as you know, they generate priority fees for the network that pays for everything else including cheap transfers when you pay a friend with USDC. So, uh, we have we have that dedi- uh, on- on Day 2. And then of course the developer stage, uh, uh, at the Lock-In. And then Day 3 is, uh, a dedicated communi- community stage on Lock-In. And, um, here we'll- we're going to have all of the rest of Solana's, uh, application layer featured in its full glory. Um, just the one innovation we made this year was, and we're open- you know, we'd love your feedback at the end of all of this, is we partnered with our, uh, colleagues from these, uh, allied organizations to do the programming. This is consistent with the Foundation's spirit to, uh, you know, decentralize more of what we do on a day-to-day basis and hand it over to the community. And so this was a collaboration on the Lock-In Stage. Today's programming is a collaboration with bi- the wonderful folks at Bitwise and Expaam. Raoul Pal, if you're here, thank you very much. Uh, as well as to the awesome Bitwise crew for pulling that together. And then on Day 3, we have a community stage that has been programmed, uh, in partnership with Solflare, who also happen to be one of our sponsors. So thank you won- to the wonderful folks at Solflare. Please give them a round of applause. They put in a lot of work to do this.

All right. Uh, getting around. There's some fun spaces here. Uh, um, as you know, you know, the- the one of the joys of working with an exceptional team is that we don't have to do much. I per- I certainly don't. And, you know, I have the joy and honor of working with someone like Ellie who's put together a bunch of exceptional experiences for you in collaboration with the ecosystem teams. Give her a shou- a round of applause. If you have met her, you know that she's a force of nature. And if you haven't, you should do that right away. Um, some- some of my favorite spaces. We have a Solana History Wall. This is five years of Solana. So starting from 201- 17 through 2019, we have key moments that all of you will recognize. Uh, and to match that, we have a Merch Corner. So depending on when you got into Solana, you can get a heat press of some of the, uh, you know, iconic moments from those years on your merch. Um, we also have Inverse Cramer, uh, appropriately named because it's a media zone. This is something we take seriously. We want everybody here to be the CMO of Solana. There's- Solana has thousands of CMOs. You're all marketers. Uh, and there is nothing more genuine than, uh, the founders, developers, and teams talking about Solana versus any of us doing it. So please go there and you have the opportunity to create more media. Um, and then we have, uh, uh, you know, if you want to look handsome like me, we have Baldpoint, uh, and which is the official haircut of, uh, Solana Breakpoint. And you can go there at Solana Spaces, uh, and, uh, and get one yourself. And of course, we have an awesome wellness space, uh, hosted by Cudis, uh, and this- and, uh, Moonwalk, which are a growing set of applications focused on marrying fitness and crypto. So, uh, please go check these spaces out. This is an experiential, uh, event and it's not, you know, uh, one of the run-of-the-mill conferences where you just have a bunch of booths that, uh, do nothing.

And then you have this map. This is pasted everywhere. It is a little bit confusing to get around the venue. So you want to have access to this map when you can. Uh, if you scan that QR code, it should get you a copy of that on your phone. It's also there in pretty much every major part of the venue. So make sure you have one of these so you don't get lost and if you need to get- be in a certain place at a certain time, uh, you have access to this.

With that, we'll close with, uh, a final... note. Um, okay. Well, this is not a conference. So we were thinking of what it is, right? And what is it? And for me, and I- I- I- it is honestly f- this is my fifth Breakpoint and it's a big family reunion for me. For some of us old people who've been around for this long, it's awesome to see our friends, people we work with, people we debate with, people we compete with, all show up at the same place and have this camaraderie which is so special because this is what makes Solana what it is. And, um, you know, the first Breakpoint, I, uh, uh, I met my f- I made my first friend, which is Chase Barker. You know, went up and said hi. Biggest regret of my life. Uh, shout out Chase. Um, but that got me into the ecosystem- there he is. Uh, that got me into the ecosystem. You know, I- I spoke at my third Breakpoint. I organized the fourth and fifth one. And that shows you that you can- you- you can just participate in an open source community and, uh, do, uh, you know, do your bit to contribute to the network and the network will reward you back. And so take a moment to look at the people around you here. Those people building the hundred million dollar startups, that graph, those people are sitting here. And the graph we'll show in five years from now of people building billion dollar revenue startups are also sitting here. So, you know, my, uh, my advice would be to just look left, look right, and try and talk to somebody next to you because, uh, I've met- made lifelong friends this way and found work that's very meaningful and I hope you do the same.

Uh, yeah. For me also... Uh, for me, this is my fifth Breakpoint. It's my 11th year in crypto. I got into this back in 2014 when this was not hot, this was not popular, this was a very suspect thing to be doing. Uh, because I thought that this idea that you could build native financial infrastructure for the internet, five and a half billion people, uh, simple computing device and you can access financial rails and at a click of a button you can access financial system whether you're in New York or whether you are, uh, in a village in, uh, in anywhere in the- anywhere else in the world. And so I- that was the really the vision that motivated me. And for a long time I thought that's just going to be on a white paper. Uh, that's never really going to be hap- that's never going to happen until I found Solana. Uh, a lot of people, you know, know that Solana has good tech. It's fast and it's cheap, it's performant. Uh, but we get asked a lot, what- what else is it that kind of makes Solana special? You know, there's other chains that are- can be faster, can be cheaper. Uh, and really what it is is, you know, we are a proof of stake chain, but we are a proof of work community. Uh, and it really is the culture and the community that makes Solana what it is. Uh, we always root for one another. We want each other to win. We cheer each other's successes. Uh, we don't have a central character in Solana. Solana is the central charac- character. The community is the central character because Solana is everyone here, right? Solana is you. Uh, and so that is, uh, where we are headed. And also just as a hat tip to where we came from, another video for you, uh, how all of this started, which is with two coffees and a beer. Thank you so much.

Let's go back to the very beginning. Where- where does this story of Solana begin? What was the- where was the inception moment for it?

I had two coffees and a beer with a friend of mine and I was up till 4 in the morning and I had this eureka moment when I knew that I could generate a data structure permissionlessly that represented passage of time. I knew that I could build TDMA and that means I could build a fast network.

The opportunity is so big to be the Google of finance. If you're like the one place where all of finance, all markets run, that is a massive opportunity.

Like everybody's been talking about the energy across the whole set of events as the beginning of something really special.

Another successful Solana Hacker House down.

Here's what was built for Hacker House SG. What will you make in Moscow and Prague?

A meme coin known as BONK launches on Christmas Day.

What made the difference for me was Superteam. For developers, for creators, for anybody who really just wants to get involved in the space.

I personally can't imagine living in a world where crypto has a billion users and your phone is not your hardware wallet.

We converted a lot of the BONK users into Saga device holders.

IBRL is like what you're trying to do to the highways and the road systems. So you want to increase bandwidth and you want to reduce latency.

You know, Solana's been through multiple cycles. I think that's what it takes for infrastructure to be trusted for issuers, for investors, for capital allocators. And we've now finally hit that point.

Why is Solana so exciting to you?

There is this massive global community of developers who have a vested interest in helping you so that we can all succeed together.

Got the best culture, got the best community, got the best tech. Where else would you build?

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: Breakpoint 2025 details are solid, highlighting ecosystem growth, global expansion, and developer interest but lacks specific product or protocol announcements.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a colossal supercomputing core as the Semantic Anchor because the story is about Solana's evolution into 'industrial-grade' infrastructure with massive throughput (3.3 Tbps), explicitly compared to the UAE's real-world supercomputing and data center facilities. This anchor visualizes the fusion of raw power, data flow, and immense scale. I included the text '3.3 Tbps' as a holographic diagnostic readout on the core itself, making it a diegetic part of the machine that directly quotes the headline's key metric.
visual_idea.description: A towering, cylindrical supercomputing core dominates the vertical frame, its surface a complex lattice of brushed aluminum, polished chrome heatsinks, and translucent panels. Thousands of fiber optic cables, glowing with brilliant cyan and magenta light, snake across its surface and plunge into its depths. In the center, the text '3.3 Tbps' is projected as a crisp, holographic diagnostic readout, part of the machine's interface. The core is housed in a cavernous, dark data center hall, with rows of server racks receding into the atmospheric haze, emphasizing colossal scale.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A towering, cylindrical supercomputing core dominates the vertical frame (3:4 aspect ratio), its colossal surface a complex lattice of meticulously brushed aluminum, highly polished chrome heatsinks exhibiting subtle reflections and deep shadows, and translucent panels. Thousands of fiber optic cables, glowing intensely with brilliant cyan and magenta light, snake elegantly across its intricate surface and plunge into its mysterious depths. In the absolute center, the text '3.3 Tbps' is projected as a crisp, holographic diagnostic readout, floating with luminous clarity as an integral part of the machine's advanced interface. The monumental core is housed in a cavernous, dark data center hall, with seemingly endless rows of server racks receding into the atmospheric haze, creating a profound sense of colossal scale and emphasizing its towering height. The scene is bathed in dramatic, volumetric lighting, enhancing the epic scale and deep shadows. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, ultra high definition, strong contrast.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a colossal supercomputing core as the Semantic Anchor because the story is about Solana's evolution into 'industrial-grade' infrastructure with massive throughput (3.3 Tbps), explicitly compared to the UAE's real-world supercomputing and data center facilities. This anchor visualizes the fusion of raw power, data flow, and immense scale. I included the text '3.3 Tbps' as a holographic diagnostic readout on the core itself, making it a diegetic part of the machine that directly quotes the headline's key metric.
visual_idea.description: A tight, macro shot focusing on a junction point on the supercomputing core. A thick bundle of intensely glowing cyan fiber optic cables connects to a gleaming, polished chrome input port. The intricate engineering is visible, with micro-etchings on the metal surface. Heat haze subtly shimmers in the air, conveying immense energy and processing power.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A tight, macro shot focusing on a junction point on the supercomputing core. A thick bundle of intensely glowing cyan fiber optic cables connects to a gleaming, polished chrome input port, featuring hyper-reflective surfaces that catch crisp, subtle reflections. The intricate engineering is visible, with ultra-detailed micro-etchings on the metal surface. Heat haze subtly shimmers in the air, conveying immense energy and processing power, intensified by strong volumetric lighting effects, with subtle light rays and atmospheric haze. The intensely glowing cyan fiber optic cables emit a vibrant light that volumetrically scatters. Composed for a square aspect ratio (1:1), emphasizing perfect symmetry and a central, tight macro focus. The frame is entirely filled with intricate texture and detail. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, realistic materials, dramatic shadow falloff.

editorial.headline: Solmate and RockawayX Merge to Create $2B Giant as Solana Unveils 3.3 Tbps Capacity
editorial.standfirst: The ecosystem pivots from experimental infrastructure to industrial-grade throughput with the rollout of DoubleZero and Jito’s Block Assembly Marketplace. This maturation is headlined by the formation of Solmate, a $2 billion institutional liquidity engine designed to bridge DeFi and public markets.
key_takeaways: Solmate merges with RockawayX to form a $2 billion asset manager led by Marco Santori and Viktor Fischer.
Network capacity hits 3.3 terabits per second via DoubleZero, with 40% of validators already onboarded.
Physical Staked ETFs captured $1 billion AUM in just six weeks, validating institutional demand.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
If previous Breakpoints were about potential, Breakpoint 2025 is about profit. The theme "Revenue and Returns" isn't just marketing; it's a structural pivot verified by the data. The headline event is the consolidation of capital power: **Solmate** (ticker SLMT) has merged with **RockawayX** in an all-stock deal, creating a combined entity with over **$2 billion** in assets. This isn't just a treasury play; by uniting **Marco Santori**'s regulatory acumen with **Viktor Fischer**'s infrastructure operations, the new entity creates a "flywheel" of validator income, venture capital, and on-chain liquidity that rivals traditional prime brokers.

Underpinning this capital formation is a hardened network layer that has finally shed its "beta" label. The **Agave** client proved its worth during the October 10 market crash, processing **100,000 transactions per second** and 60 million compute units per block while centralized exchanges faltered. But the real alpha is **DoubleZero**, a "Layer 0.5" bandwidth network founded by **Austin Federa**. By securing **3.3 terabits per second** of dedicated capacity—bypassing the bottlenecks of the public internet—Solana has built the fiber-optic rails necessary for high-frequency TradFi trading. With **40% of the network** already routing through DoubleZero, the "latency wars" are being won on-chain.

This infrastructure maturity is directly correlating with institutional inflows. **Physical Staked ETFs** have absorbed **$1 billion in AUM** in a mere six weeks, with major players like **Western Union** ($100B volume) and **Fiserv** ($2T volume) now actively building on the network. As **Jito** rolls out its **Block Assembly Marketplace (BAM)** to give applications precise control over transaction sequencing, Solana has effectively transitioned from a speculative casino to the "Silicon Valley of the Internet"—a place where revenue-generating businesses like **Drift** and **MetaDAO** are shipping code that compounds capital, not just hype.
